title: scrophularia scorodonia var. glabrescens
intro: Scrophularia scorodonia var. glabrescens, also known as the scorpion gentian, is a variety of the Scrophularia scorodonia plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the figwort family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Eastern Europe and Russia, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Scrophularia scorodonia var. glabrescens can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
